title: alopecurus indicus
intro: Alopecurus indicus, also known as Indian foxtail grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Alopecurus indicus include its ability to lower blood sugar levels, reduce fever, and aid in digestion. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various health conditions.
